description: Ratio of products that have adapters, sample indices, UMIs, R1 and R2 in the expected order and map to the same chromosome within ~500bp
Rationale/reference: Based on correct product ratio observed in CODECseq paper

description: Alignment rate of processed reads
Rationale/reference: Higher alignment rates indicate high quality reads

description: Percentage of reads that map to multiple genomic locations
Rationale/reference: Reads should almost all map to only one position

description: Mean insert size (start of R1 to end of R2) as determined by alignment (bp)
Rationale/reference: 150bp gives ideal overlap, 300 gives no overlap and 50 gives 50bp overlap

description: Alignment rate of processed reads
Rationale/reference: Higher alignment rates indicate high quality reads

description: Percentage of reads that map to multiple genomic locations
Rationale/reference: Reads should almost all map to only one position

description: Percentage of reads marked as duplicates
Rationale/reference: Duplication rates are typically ~3-7% with PCR-free prep

description: Peak of insert size distribution as determined by alignment (bp)
Rationale/reference: Illumina PCR-free prep should yield mean insert size 350-550bp depending on size selection

description: Tally of the total number of variants called (x10^6  variants)
Rationale/reference: Does the number of called variants agrees with the number of expected germline variants in the population (TABLE 1: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9439720/)

description: Calculate the ratio of SNV's / INDELS
Rationale/reference: Does the SNV/INDEL ratio agree with the expected population ratio (TABLE 1: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9439720/)

description: Assess whether the IN/DEL ratio agress with the literature
Rationale/reference: TABLE 1: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9439720/

description: Number of MNP / OTHER variants after decomposition
Rationale/reference: MNP/OTHER should be converted to SNVs/INDELs

description: Assess whether the Ti/Tv ratio agrees with the expected ratio
Rationale/reference: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/bimj.202300278

description: Assess whether the Het/Hom ratio agrees with the expected ratio
Rationale/reference: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/bimj.202300278

description: Percent of somatic SNVs called that have >1x alt depth
Rationale/reference: Most somatic mutations are expected to be very low VAF (<0.1%), so positions with >1x alt depth should be very rare

description: Percent of variant calls with somatic SNVs called within 150bp of any soft clipping event
Rationale/reference: Any variant called near soft clipping is at high risk of being due to incorrect alignment/contamination

description: Percentage of library that is flagged as UMI duplicates
Rationale/reference: Based on duplication rate observed in CODECseq paper

description: Percentage of reads which successfully aligned during DSC realignment
Percentage of reads with a mapQ score of at least 60
Total duplex bases in include_bed region divided by total positions in include_bed region
Rationale/reference: Ideally >1x depth for good coverage, but max depth depends on experimental aims. To be considered a duplex base, requires >=Q70.

description: Percentage of genome eligible for variant calling (duplex depth > 0 & unmasked)
Rationale/reference: Will be lower than bedregions coverage. To be considered a duplex base, requires >=Q70.

description: Percentage of genome with duplex depth > 0
Rationale/reference: Will be lower than bedregions coverage. To be considered a duplex base, requires >=Q70.

description: Rate at which Watson and Crick complementary bases disagree (total R1R2 disagreements divided by total duplex bases for each sample)
Rationale/reference: nn_upper of 8e-4 = ~160 false positive somatic mutations, ideal_lower = ~18 false positive somatic mutations.

description: gDNA required for quantitation and standard Illumina sequencing by AGRF (ng)
Rationale/reference: 50ng for gel + 600ng requested by AGRF, more gives buffer

description: Variability in gDNA obtained from samples of the same type (normalised IQR)
Rationale/reference: 0.2 is a common target for assay development

description: gDNA fragment sizes required for standard Illumina sequencing by AGRF (bp)
Rationale/reference: Illumina recommends fragment sizes of at least 10kb, 20kb+ is ideal for tagmentation

description: gDNA required for CODECseq library preparation (ng)
Rationale/reference: 1000ng is absolute minimum for CODECseq + 50ng for gel, more gives buffer

description: Yield of fragmented DNA required for quantitation and rest of CODECseq library preparation (ng)
Rationale/reference: 70ng is absolute minimum, more gives buffer

description: Variability in fragmented DNA yield between samples (normalised IQR)
Rationale/reference: 0.2 is a common target for assay development

description: Fragment sizes of DNA inserts following enzymatic fragmentation and size selection (bp)
Rationale/reference: 150bp gives ideal overlap, 300 gives no overlap and 50 gives 50bp overlap

description: Yield of amplified DNA required for quantitation and sequencing by AGRF (ng, assuming 650bp fragment size and 4 samples per lane)
Rationale/reference: 85ng is absolute minimum, more gives buffer

description: Variability in amplified DNA yield between samples (normalised IQR)
Rationale/reference: 0.2 is a common target for assay development

description: Fragment sizes of library following amplification (bp)
Rationale/reference: 570bp gives ideal overlap, 870bp gives no overlap and 370bp gives 50bp overlap

description: Fragment sizes of undiluted no template control after amplification
Rationale/reference: Defined bands at 284 (quadruplex) and 568bp (quadruplex dimer) without smears. Band at ~400bp may also be present.

description: Percentage of reference genome masked by combined mask
Rationale/reference: Even if only masking germline variants, percent masked should be at least 0.1%

description: Peak in mean sequence quality across r1 reads (Phred score)
Rationale/reference: Stricter than fastqc warnings due to requirement for increased accuracy

description: Peak in mean sequence quality across r2 reads (Phred score)
Rationale/reference: Stricter than fastqc warnings due to requirement for increased accuracy

description: Lower quartile of IQR for base quality at each position in reads (Phred score)

description: Peak of read length distribution (bp)
Rationale/reference: Reads should all be 150bp (if requesting 150PE)

description: Difference in the percentage of total read pairs that belong to each sample
Rationale/reference: If products are correctly formed in all samples, the difference in total reads within the pool should be small

description: Total number of reads obtained from standard Illumina sequencing by AGRF (x million reads)
Rationale/reference: nn based on 1B lane, ideal based on 3B lane

description: Total number of reads obtained from standard Illumina sequencing by AGRF (x million reads)

Rationale/reference: Typically read pairs are filtered if read length <70bp

description: Percentage of remaining reads filtered due to low mean quality score
Rationale/reference: Typically read pairs are filtered if mean quality score is <36x
